title: DNA extraction optimisation for minute land snails of the genus Vertigo O.F. Müller, 1773 (Gastropoda: Vertiginidae): a comparative evaluation of six methods including a non-destructive shell-preserving protocol
author: Jukonis, Benediktas
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This dataset contains the summary data underlying all tables and figures in the manuscript "DNA extraction optimisation for minute land snails of the genus Vertigo O.F. Muller, 1773 (Gastropoda: Vertiginidae): a comparative evaluation of six methods including a non-destructive shell-preserving protocol" submitted to Molecular Ecology Resources. Six DNA extraction methods were compared using Vertigo antivertigo preserved in 96% ethanol (n = 10 per method). Metrics include DNA concentration (QuantiFluor dsDNA), spectrophotometric purity (NanoDrop A260/280 and A260/230), PCR amplification success across four loci (COI, COI mini, ITS1, ITS2), Bioanalyzer fragment analysis in the 200-1000 bp window, and cost-per-result estimates. The workbook contains README, Table1_Methods, Table2_Yield_Purity, Table3_PCR_Success, Table4_Cost, Table5_Bioanalyzer, and GenBank_Sequences sheets. Representative ITS1 sequences are deposited in GenBank under accessions PZ227160-PZ227162. Specimens were collected under permit No. 600000-S-55 (Lithuanian Environmental Protection Agency, 2020-06-08).
